column3: The activity that brought the physical painting into existence (workshop/manufacture making).

This model captures the production event that resulted in the physical painting. It records who carried it out, where and when it occurred, how it is identified and described, and the documentary sources that explicitly evidence the event. The model links the production to the painting(s) produced and distinguishes descriptive notes from documentary evidence to support provenance and scholarly validation.
The physical artwork produced by this event.
The actor (artist, studio, workshop) who carried out the production.
The place where the production activity occurred.
Documents that explicitly evidence or discuss the production event (e.g., contracts, workshop records, technical reports, catalogues).
One or more values from a controlled list (e.g., painted, printed, cast).
